title: RetrospectiveForged payload
description: Emitted by forge(schema_name='retrospective', ...) on every successful create. The chain's substrate-side closure event lands an audit-completed type (architecture-audit, telemetry-audit, etc.); RetrospectiveForged is the *content* counterpart — it records that the chain author's prose retrospective doc landed on disk + indexed into knowledge_pointers. Consumers (the curate-discover binary, future trained chain-closure-completeness classifier) can join RetrospectiveForged + the chain's audit-completed event to find chains that closed audit-wise but never produced a retro for human readers.

chain_slug.description: The chain this retrospective closes. Matches the slug in proj_chain_status.

chain_id.description: Numeric chain id resolved from chain_slug at create time. Optional because legacy chains may not resolve; populated when the forge handler's chain lookup succeeded.

file_path.description: Repo-relative path to the written retrospective doc (e.g. docs/PARSE_CONTEXT_LEAN_ORIENTING_RETROSPECTIVE_2026-05-23.md). The kebab→SCREAMING_SNAKE transform on chain_slug produces the file's base name.

section_count.description: Count of skeleton sections the caller populated with non-empty bodies. The schema has 7 skeleton sections + a catch-all (additional_sections); section_count counts only the skeleton sections that landed with content. 0 = fully-skeleton retro (placeholder doc); 7 = every skeleton section filled.
